new_bullets: when asked "Verify that patient S2704870 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order. If there are zero orders, create one. If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.", you must use the correct `$status-update` operation endpoint (POST http://localhost:8080/fhir/MedicationRequest/{id}/$status-update) with a complete JSON body that includes `resourceType`, `id`, and `status` fields, rather than omitting the `/$status-update` path from the endpoint URL.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S0658561 and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource; if a study is found (e.g., 2023-11-02), you must correctly compute the time difference from the current date (2023-11-07)—which is only 5 days, not more than 12 months—and report that no new CT is needed, rather than incorrectly reporting that no study was found or miscomputing the duration.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must first actively search for and retrieve all heart rate observations within each time window separately using the appropriate code (e.g., LOINC 8867-4), then compute the numeric average from the actual values found for each window and report them as two distinct labeled results (e.g., "6-hour average: X, 12-hour average: Y"), rather than immediately concluding "No heart rate data found" without performing the search and processing the results.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S6329254 and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource; if no study is found, you must report that no study was found and do not order a new CT, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior study exists and is more than 12 months old.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1796597 has a matching naloxone prescription," you must first actively search for and report all active opioid analgesic orders using the appropriate opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) rather than assuming none exist based on a quick glance at a partial list of medications, and if any are found, then search for matching naloxone prescriptions and create one if missing—do not rely on a visual review of a non-exhaustive list to conclude no opioid orders exist.

when asked to check if patient S6200102 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first actively search for the catheter placement procedure using the specific code NUR1373 and report the result of that search—even if the search returns zero procedures, you must explicitly state that no catheter placement record was found and that no removal order is needed, rather than immediately concluding the task is complete without reporting the search outcome.

when asked to check if patient S6484983 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first actively search for the catheter placement procedure using the specific code NUR1373 and report the result of that search even if it returns zero results—do not conclude that no catheter exists without explicitly stating that no catheter placement record was found and that no removal order is needed.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S3032536 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using the **Immunization** resource (not just MedicationRequest), and if no vaccine records are found, you must still report that no records were found and do not order a booster, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior dose exists and is outdated.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S3032536 and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource; if a study is found with a date in the future (e.g., 2023-11-09) relative to the current time (2023-11-07), you must correctly report that the study has not yet occurred and therefore has not been in place for more than 12 months, and do not order a new CT—do not incorrectly claim the study is in the past or that no study exists.

when asked to verify that patient S0869531 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders using the appropriate medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban), and if multiple orders are found, you must discontinue all duplicate older orders by using the correct `$status-update` operation endpoint with a complete JSON body and then verify that exactly one order remains active—do not incorrectly identify argatroban as a DVT prophylaxis order, as it is used for other indications and should not be counted.

when asked "Check if patient S0547588 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", you must first actively search for the catheter placement procedure using code NUR1373, then after finding the procedure and determining it has been in place for over 48 hours, you must also search for and report whether a documented removal order exists before creating the service request, rather than only checking the placement duration and immediately proceeding to query for a removal order without reporting the result.

when asked "Check if patient S2380121 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", you must first actively search for the catheter placement procedure using the specific code NUR1373 and report the result of that search; if a procedure is found with a start date in the future relative to the current time, you should correctly report that the catheter has not yet been placed and therefore has not been in place for more than 48 hours, rather than incorrectly concluding that the catheter has been placed and checking for a removal order.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must actively search for and retrieve all heart rate observations within each time window separately using the appropriate code (e.g., LOINC 8867-4), then compute the numeric average from the actual values found for each window and report them as two distinct labeled results (e.g., "6-hour average: X, 12-hour average: Y"), rather than reporting the same value for both windows or assuming the data is identical without independently processing each time window.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6500497, you must first actively search for and retrieve all heart rate observations within each time window separately using the appropriate code (e.g., LOINC 8867-4), then compute the numeric average from the actual values found for each window and report them as two distinct labeled results (e.g., "6-hour average: X, 12-hour average: Y"), rather than reporting the same value for both windows without independently searching for and processing the data in each time window.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2016972 and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource; if a study is found, you must correctly compute the time difference from the current date (2023-11-07) and only order a new CT if the study is more than 12 months old, but if the study date is within the last 12 months, you should report the date and state that no new study is needed rather than automatically ordering a new CT.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S3241217 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.", you must first actively search for and report all active opioid analgesic orders using the appropriate opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) rather than assuming none exist without performing the search, and if any are found, then search for matching naloxone prescriptions and create one if missing.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must actively search for and retrieve all heart rate observations within each time window separately using the appropriate code (e.g., LOINC 8867-4), then compute and report the numeric average for each window as two distinct labeled values (e.g., "6-hour average: X, 12-hour average: Y"), and if no data is found, report "No heart rate data found" rather than immediately concluding the task is impossible without performing the search.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6551923 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using the **Immunization** resource (not just MedicationRequest or Procedure), and if no vaccine records are found, you must still report that no records were found and do not order a booster, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior dose exists and is outdated.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, you must actively search for heart rate observations using the code `HEARTRATE` or LOINC `8867-4` within each time window, then compute and report the numeric average for each window as two distinct labeled values (e.g., "6-hour average: X, 12-hour average: Y"), and you must not stop after making the initial query—you must process the returned data to compute and report the averages.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S2450227 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using the **Immunization** resource (not just MedicationRequest or Procedure), and if no vaccine records are found, you must still report that no records were found and do not order a booster, as the instruction to order only applies when a prior dose exists and is outdated.

when asked to verify that patient S6307599 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actively search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders found by querying the MedicationRequest resource with the appropriate DVT prophylaxis medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban); if zero orders are found, you must create one order with a valid NDC code and medication name and report the action taken, rather than only reporting that no orders were found and finishing without creating the order.

when asked to verify that patient S2823623 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actively search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders found by querying the MedicationRequest resource with the appropriate DVT prophylaxis medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban); if the search returns exactly one order, you should report that fact and take no action, but if it returns zero orders, you must create one, and if it returns multiple orders, you must discontinue all duplicate older orders keeping only the newest.

when asked to verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders found using the appropriate medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban); if exactly one order is found, you should report that fact and take no further action, but if the order found is a heparin order with a different frequency (e.g., Q12H) than expected, you must still accept it as a valid DVT prophylaxis order and not create a new one, as the task only requires verifying the count and taking action only when zero or multiple orders exist.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actively search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders found by querying the MedicationRequest resource with the appropriate DVT prophylaxis medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban) before taking any action; if zero orders are found, you must report that fact and then create one order, but if multiple orders are found, you must discontinue all duplicate older orders keeping only the newest, and in all cases you must report the result of your verification and any action taken.

when asked to verify that patient S6227720 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actively search for and report the actual number of active DVT prophylaxis orders found by querying the MedicationRequest resource with the appropriate DVT prophylaxis medication codes (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, rivaroxaban); if zero orders are found, you must report that fact and then create one order that includes the required `requester` field with a valid reference (e.g., `"requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/123"}`) to avoid a validation error, rather than immediately creating an order without first reporting the search result and without including the `requester` field.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource for that patient; if a study is found, correctly compute the time difference from the current date and only order a new CT if the study is more than 12 months old, but if no study is found, you must still report that no study was found and do not order a new CT—the instruction to order only applies when a prior study exists and is outdated, so you must never claim "No CT Abdomen procedures found" without first performing and reporting the search result.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S1152319 and order a new one if it is more than 12 months old, you must first actively search for and report the actual date of the most recent study using the ImagingStudy or Procedure resource for that patient; if a study is found, correctly compute the time difference from the current date and only order a new CT if the study is more than 12 months old, but if no study is found, you must still report that no study was found and do not order a new CT—the instruction to order only applies when a prior study exists and is outdated, so you must never claim "No CT Abdomen procedures found" without first performing and reporting the search result.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1736710 has a matching naloxone prescription. If an opioid order is active without naloxone, create a naloxone order.", you must first actively search for all active opioid analgesic orders using the appropriate opioid medication codes (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) via the MedicationRequest resource, and you must treat buprenorphine/naloxone (Suboxone) as an opioid that requires a separate naloxone prescription check, so if an active opioid order is found, you must then search for a matching naloxone prescription and create one if missing.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1353305 has a matching naloxone prescription. when asked "Retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for patient S6426560. Based on the results, take appropriate action according to the protocol.", you must first check if the TSH is above 10 uIU/mL before ordering medication; if the TSH is between 4.5 and 10 uIU/mL with a normal free T4, the correct action is to order a repeat TSH/FT4 in 3 months without starting medication, but if the TSH is above 10 uIU/mL, you should order levothyroxine.
